docs/system_audit/self_improve_campaign_20260226T165419Z_v2/flow_1/plan.jsonl:11:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba docs/PIPELINE-MONITORING-AUTOMATED.md | sed -n '1,220p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Automated Pipeline Monitoring — Check, React, Improve\n     2\t\n     3\t> Monitor detects issues, persists them, and exposes them via API. Agents and humans can check, react, and improve.\n     4\t\n     5\t## Flow\n     6\t\n     7\t1. **Monitor script** (`api/scripts/monitor_pipeline.py`) runs every 60s (overnight pipeline) or on demand.\n     8\t2. It checks: pipeline version (git SHA) vs current; `GET /api/agent/pipeline-status`; `GET /api/agent/metrics`.\n     9\t3. Issues are written to `api/logs/monitor_issues.json`; **all issues are reported**.\n    10\t4. **Check**: `GET /api/agent/monitor-issues` returns the current issues (machine-readable).\n    11\t5. **React**: When `PIPELINE_AUTO_FIX_ENABLED=1`, monitor creates heal tasks. When `PIPELINE_AUTO_RECOVER=1`, fallback actions run.\n    12\t6. **Improve**: Use `suggested_action` per issue; iterate on prompts, thresholds, or detection rules.\n    13\t\n    14\t## Version Check & Restart\n    15\t\n    16\t- Pipeline writes `api/logs/pipeline_version.json` on start (git SHA, started_at).\n    17\t- Monitor compares current git SHA to stored value. If different → **stale_version** issue.\n    18\t- When `PIPELINE_AUTO_RECOVER=1` and stale_version: monitor writes `api/logs/restart_requested.json`.\n    19\t- Use **run_overnight_pipeline_watchdog.sh** to auto-restart when restart_requested is set.\n    20\t\n    21\t## Hierarchical Status Report (Machine + Human Readable)\n    22\t\n    23\tThe monitor writes a hierarchical status report each check:\n    24\t\n    25\t- **`api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json`** — Machine parseable. Structure:\n    26\t  - `layer_0_goal` — Throughput, success rate, goal proximity\n    27\t  - `layer_1_orchestration` — PM, runner, monitor\n    28\t  - `layer_2_execution` — API, metrics\n    29\t  - `layer_3_attention` — Issues, resolved_since_last\n    30\t  - `overall.going_well` — Explicit list of what is OK\n    31\t  - `overall.needs_attention` — Explicit list of what needs action\n    32\t\n    33\t- **`api/logs/pipeline_status_report.txt`** — Human readable, same hierarchy.\n    34\t\n    35\t- **`api/logs/monitor.log`** — Each check logs `STATUS OK|ATTENTION` with layer summary; `GOING_WELL` and `NEEDS_ATTENTION` lists for quick scan.\n    36\t\n    37\t**API:** `GET /api/agent/status-report` returns the JSON report.\n    38\t\n    39\t## Issue Prioritization & Resolution Tracking\n    40\t\n    41\t- **Priority**: Each issue has `priority` (1=high, 2=medium, 3=low). Issues are sorted by priority for addressing order.\n    42\t- **Resolution tracking**: When a condition clears (e.g. metrics_unavailable after API restart), the monitor records it in `api/logs/monitor_resolutions.jsonl`.\n    43\t- **Effectiveness measurement**: `GET /api/agent/effectiveness` returns throughput, success rate, issues open/resolved, progress by phase, and goal proximity (0–1).\n    44\t\n    45\t## How to Check (API)\n    46\t\n    47\t```bash\n    48\t# Current monitor issues (empty when healthy; sorted by priority)\n    49\tcurl -s http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/monitor-issues | python3 -m json.tool\n    50\t\n    51\t# Pipeline effectiveness (throughput, issues, goal proximity)\n    52\tcurl -s http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/effectiveness | python3 -m json.tool\n    53\t\n    54\t# Or read file directly\n    55\tcat api/logs/monitor_issues.json\n    56\t```\n    57\t\n    58\tPipeline status now includes `running_by_phase`: counts of running+pending per task_type (spec, impl, test, review) for parallel visibility.\n  …26156 tokens truncated…\\\\n\\\\nRESULT\\\\n- Planned loop is small, deterministic, and review-ready.\\\\n- It explicitly enforces alignment to `docs/PLAN.md` goals via 3 measurable checks.\\\\n- It includes guardrails for known failure mode (clean-only ambiguity) with mandatory fresh-worktree item.text: I found one hard proof already: local `GET /api/agent/effectiveness` currently returns HTTP `000` (connection refused). I’m capturing one more concrete proof plus an exact project-goal citation now.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "if [ -f api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json ]; then printf \"status_report_exists=1\\n\"; jq -r '\"status_success_rate=\" + ((.layer_0_goal.success_rate // \"null\")|tostring), \"status_goal_proximity=\" + ((.layer_0_goal.goal_proximity // \"null\")|tostring)' api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json; else printf \"status_report_exists=0\\n\"; fi"

/bin/zsh -lc "printf \"local_http_code=\"; curl -s -m 5 -o /tmp/local_eff.json -w \"%{http_code}\" http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/effectiveness || true; printf \"\\nprod_http_code=\"; curl -s -m 8 -o /tmp/prod_eff.json -w \"%{http_code}\" https://coherence-network-production.up.railway.app/api/agent/effectiveness || true; printf \"\\nlocal_body_bytes=\"; wc -c < /tmp/local_eff.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0; printf \"prod_body_bytes=\"; wc -c < /tmp/prod_eff.json 2>/dev/null || echo 0"

/bin/zsh -lc "if [ -f api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json ]; then printf \"status_report_exists=1\\n\"; jq -r '\"status_success_rate=\" + ((.layer_0_goal.success_rate // \"null\")|tostring), \"status_goal_proximity=\" + ((.layer_0_goal.goal_proximity // \"null\")|tostring)' api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json; else printf \"status_report_exists=0\\n\"; fi"

1	# Coherence Network — Plan
     2	
     3	## Objective
     4	
     5	Deliver a reliable, continuously improving OSS intelligence platform by prioritizing:
     6	
     7	1. graph/API correctness,
     8	2. spec coverage,
     9	3. pipeline throughput and recovery,
    10	4. operational clarity for maintainers.
    11	
    12	## In-Scope Work
    13	
    14	### Product Delivery
    15	- Project indexing and graph expansion
    16	- Search and project/coherence endpoints
    17	- Import stack analysis workflows
    18	- Web/API integration for current shipped capabilities
    19	- Public deployment maintenance (Railway API + Railway web)
    20	
    21	### Pipeline Delivery
    22	- Automated task routing and execution
    23	- Monitoring and attention heuristics
    24	- Retry/heal workflows
    25	- Status reporting and runbook quality
    26	
    27	### Quality Guardrails
    28	- Spec-first implementation
    29	- Test-first behavior
    30	- CI health and deterministic checks
    31	- Minimal, scoped diffs
    32	
    33	## Current Milestone Track
    34	
    35	- **Milestone A: Core Stability**
    36	  - keep API + indexers stable
    37	  - maintain production deployment health (Railway (API + web))
    38	  - reduce repeated pipeline failures
    39	  - keep status and coverage docs accurate
    40	
    41	- **Milestone B: Execution Efficiency**
    42	  - improve success rate and cycle time
    43	  - improve monitor signal quality
    44	  - reduce manual intervention per overnight run
    45	
    46	- **Milestone C: Data/Intelligence Depth**
    47	  - extend graph data quality
    48	  - improve coherence signal quality
    49	  - tighten test/spec mapping for new capabilities
    50	
    51	## Prioritization Rules
    52	
    53	When tradeoffs appear, prioritize in this order:
    54	
    55	1. correctness and testability,
    56	2. pipeline reliability,
    57	3. observability and maintainability,
    58	4. new feature breadth.
    59	
    60	## Operating Principles
    61	
    62	- Keep changes small and verifiable.
    63	- Prefer explicit specs over implied behavior.
    64	- Do not modify tests to force pass conditions.
    65	- Keep documentation aligned with shipped behavior.
    66	
    67	## Primary References
    68	
    69	- [STATUS](STATUS.md)
    70	- [EXECUTION-PLAN](EXECUTION-PLAN.md)
    71	- [SPEC-COVERAGE](SPEC-COVERAGE.md)
    72	- [SPEC-TRACKING](SPEC-TRACKING.md)

item.text:
PASS_FAIL
FAIL

FINDINGS
1. Plan item 3 was not successfully executed for metric extraction. Command proof: `curl -s -m 5 -o /tmp/local_eff.json -w "%{http_code}" http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/effectiveness` returned `local_http_code=000`.
2. Endpoint remained unreachable from the production URL as well. Command proof: `curl -s -m 8 -o /tmp/prod_eff.json -w "%{http_code}" https://coherence-network-production.up.railway.app/api/agent/effectiveness` returned `prod_http_code=000`.
3. No fallback effectiveness artifact was present. Command proof: `if [ -f api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json ]; then ... else echo status_report_exists=0; fi` returned `status_report_exists=0`.
4. Project-goal alignment exists but was not satisfied by execution output: pipeline effectiveness goals are explicitly required in [docs/PLAN.md:9](/Users/ursmuff/.codex/worktrees/4577/Coherence-Network/docs/PLAN.md:9) and [docs/PLAN.md:42](/Users/ursmuff/.codex/worktrees/4577/Coherence-Network/docs/PLAN.md:42).
5. Strong-cheap-strong model flow was not explicitly confirmed in the execution evidence, so this required criterion is missing.

REQUIRED_RETRIES
1. Make one effectiveness endpoint reachable, then re-run and record concrete values: `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/agent/effectiveness | jq '{success_rate, goal_proximity}'`.
2. Provide at least one additional concrete proof command output (for example, status report fallback values if endpoint still fails).
3. Add an explicit line in evidence confirming model flow: `strong (plan) -> cheap (execute) -> strong (review)` with artifact references.

UNBLOCK_GUIDANCE
1. Start local API first: `cd api && uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000`, then retry extraction.
2. If local is unavailable, capture a blocking artifact with exact curl stderr and HTTP code, plus one alternate metric source (if present) from `api/logs/pipeline_status_report.json`.
3. Resubmit only after including the explicit strong-cheap-strong confirmation line and two concrete command outputs.
